reasoning: In this simulated scenario, the current date is April 2026. Other EU NATO countries, such as Romania, also have explicit plans for major purchases (including F-35s and combat vehicles) using their share of the loans. Since the loans allow procurement from non-European suppliers and the funds are unlocking right now (spring 2026), there is a very high probability that at least two European NATO nations will formally sign binding contracts surpassing the €1 billion threshold before the July 31, 2026 deadline.
